key_metrics_output: Particle counts, polymer type identification, particle size, match score (spectral quality), minimum detectable amount (MDA), microplastic concentration per liter
abstract: Standard operating procedures for extraction and measurement of microplastic particles in treated drinking water using sieving, vacuum filtration, visual microscopy for particle counting, and Raman spectroscopy for chemical identification. The method reliably detects microplastic particles ≥20 μm in size, with a maximum size of 5,000 μm. Samples are separated into size fractions (500 μm, 212 μm, 20 μm), and a representative subset of particles is chemically identified using Raman spectroscopy. The proportion of particles confirmed as microplastics is applied to total microscopic counts to estimate microplastic concentration per liter. Minimum detectable amounts range from 47–88 particles depending on size fraction.

particle_size_range: The lowest particle size reliably detected by this method is 20 µm, with a maximum size of 5,000 µm
